title: Sample Preparation Protocol for Total Proteomic Analysis of Mouse Tissues
author: Raja S. Nirujogi
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bs3tngnn
materials: Pool all samples into a single tube.

steps.title: Snap freeze mouse tissue immediately after...
steps.instruction: Snap freeze mouse tissue immediately after isolation in liquid nitrogen and store at -80 °C .

steps.title: Pulverize frozen tissue in liquid nitrogen...
steps.instruction: Pulverize frozen tissue in liquid nitrogen to a fine powder and snap freeze immediately in 2 ml tubes. This is done using cell crusher kit. Note: Maintain all Pulverizer units in liquid nitrogen including spatula. After use wash the pulveriser with a tap water and clean thoroughly several times with 70 %, (by vol) ethanol .

steps.title: Weigh pulverized tissue powder on a...
steps.instruction: Weigh pulverized tissue powder on a weighing balance (5-7% of the weight should be protein i.e. ~ 5 to 7 mg protein/100 mg). Note: Keep samples on dry ice during after weighing. Take a 1.5 ml tube, check the weight on weighing balance and subsequently tare the weight. Now take small scoop of sample using pre-chilled fine spatula to weigh the sample).

steps.title: Immediately add 10 times of the...
steps.instruction: Immediately add 10 times of the weight of the tissue of 2 %, (by mass) SDS lysis buffer . Note: For 100 mg, tissue powder please add 1000 µL, lysis buffer .

steps.title: Put samples on an orbital shaker...
steps.instruction: Put samples on an orbital shaker in cold room at 1000 rpm, °C, 15 minutes .

steps.title: Boil samples at 95 °C for...
steps.instruction: Boil samples at 95 °C for 00:10:00 and allow to cool.

steps.title: Sonicate samples using a Diagenode Bioruptor...
steps.instruction: Sonicate samples using a Diagenode Bioruptor (use it at high energy for 10 cycles (30Sec-ON/30Sec-Off).

steps.title: Clarify lysate by centrifuging at 20800...
steps.instruction: Clarify lysate by centrifuging at 20800 x g, 4 °C, 30 minutes .

steps.title: Measure protein amount using the Bicinchoninic...
steps.instruction: Measure protein amount using the Bicinchoninic acid assay (BCA) method in triplicate at 1 to 10 and 1 to 20 dilutions - repeat analysis if readings are not close.

steps.title: Take 200 µg, protein for total proteomic analysis
steps.instruction: Take 200 µg, protein for total proteomic analysis.

steps.title: Perform reduction by adding a 1...
steps.instruction: Perform reduction by adding a 1 in 10 dilution of a solution of 0.1 Molarity (M), TCEP dissolved in 300 millimolar (mM), TEABC to bring final concentration of TCEP to 10 millimolar (mM) .

steps.title: Incubate on a Thermomixer for 00:30:00...
steps.instruction: Incubate on a Thermomixer for 00:30:00 at 60 °C with a gentle agitation at 1000 rpm, 23 °C, .

steps.title: Bring tubes to Room temperature and...
steps.instruction: Bring tubes to Room temperature and add a one in 10 dilution of freshly prepared 0.4 Molarity (M), iodoacetamide dissolved in water. Note: It is critical that the samples are at Room temperature prior to addition of iodoacetamide.

steps.title: Incubate in dark on a Thermomixer...
steps.instruction: Incubate in dark on a Thermomixer at Room temperature for about 00:30:00 with a gentle agitation 1000 rpm, 23 °C, .

steps.title: Quench alkylation by addition of a...
steps.instruction: Quench alkylation by addition of a 1 in 10 dilution of 0.1 Molarity (M), TCEP dissolved in 300 millimolar (mM), TEABC to bring final concentration of TCEP to 10 millimolar (mM) .

steps.title: Incubate on a Thermomixer for 00:20:00...
steps.instruction: Incubate on a Thermomixer for 00:20:00 at Room temperature with a gentle agitation 1000 rpm, °C, .

steps.title: Add SDS to a final concentration...
steps.instruction: Add SDS to a final concentration of 5 %, (by mass) from 20 %, (by mass) SDS stock . Note: The lysate is already in 2 %, (by mass) SDS so supplement with a stock of 20 %, (by mass) SDS in order to bring the final SDS concentration to 5 %, (by mass) . A final concentration of 5% SDS is recommended for complete soulblization of proteins for S-Trap assisted protocol.

steps.title: Add a 1 in 10 dilution...
steps.instruction: Add a 1 in 10 dilution of 12 %, (by vol) phosphoric acid into the sample to make a final concentration of ~ 1.2 %, (by vol) phosphoric acid . Note: For example, 5 µL to 50 µL .

steps.title: Dilute the sample to in 7...
steps.instruction: Dilute the sample to in 7 times the current volume of the mixture in of S-Trap wash buffer ( 90 %, (by vol) methanol in 0.1 Molarity (M), TEAB pH 7.1 v/v) (for examples if sample volume is 50 µL add 300 µL, S-Trap wash buffer (( 90 %, (by vol) methanol in 0.1 Molarity (M), TEAB pH 7.1 v/v)). Note: If the lysate volume is now more than 50 µL , scale S-Trap wash buffer accordingly i.e. 7 times the volume of the lysate.

steps.title: Prepare an S-Trap mini column in a 2 ml tube
steps.instruction: Prepare an S-Trap mini column in a 2 ml tube.

steps.title: Add the diluted protein mixture to the column
steps.instruction: Add the diluted protein mixture to the column.

steps.title: Centrifuge briefly to capture the protein particles
steps.instruction: Centrifuge briefly to capture the protein particles. 1000 x g, 23 °C, Note: All subsequent wash steps needs to be done at room temperature (23 o C). If you observe clogging or if the sample doesn't pass through the column then increase centrifugation speed up to a maximum of 4,000g.

steps.title: Wash column with 400 µL, S-Trap...
steps.instruction: Wash column with 400 µL, S-Trap buffer a total of 4 times (spin 1000 x g, °C, 1 minute between washes). Note: The protein remains bound on the column and SDS and buffer components that effect trypsin digestion are removed.

steps.title: Move the S-Trap column to a...
steps.instruction: Move the S-Trap column to a clean 2 ml tube for digestion.

steps.title: Add a 100 µL, solution of...
steps.instruction: Add a 100 µL, solution of freshly dissolved trypsin containing 13 µg, trypsin and 2 µg, Lys-C freshly dissolved in 100 millimolar (mM), TEAB (1:15). Note: We use 6.5 µg, trypsin and 1 µg, Lys-C per 100 µg, protein .

steps.title: Centrifuge briefly at 200 x g, °C, 1 minute
steps.instruction: Centrifuge briefly at 200 x g, °C, 1 minute .

steps.title: Collect flowthrough and reapply the trypsin...
steps.instruction: Collect flowthrough and reapply the trypsin solution back onto the column being careful to avoid air bubbles.

steps.title: Cap the tubes and incubate at...
steps.instruction: Cap the tubes and incubate at 47 °C without shaking for 01:30:00 . Note: Do not shake as this causes bubbles and damages the column.

steps.title: Incubate for another 02:00:00 at Room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate for another 02:00:00 at Room temperature .

steps.title: Add 80 µL, 50 mM TEAB...
steps.instruction: Add 80 µL, 50 mM TEAB then spin to elute and place the eluate in a new 1.5 ml Eppendorf tube termed "eluate tube".

steps.title: Next, add 80 µL, 0
steps.instruction: Next, add 80 µL, 0.15% (by vol) Formic Acid and spin to elute. Also add this eluate to the "eluate tube".

steps.title: Finally, add 80 µL, 50% (by vol) Acetonitrile in 0
steps.instruction: Finally, add 80 µL, 50% (by vol) Acetonitrile in 0.15 %, (by vol) formic acid and spin to elute. Also add this eluate to the "eluate tube". Note: 3 eluates should have been added to the eluate tube.

steps.title: Take 1 µL - 2 µL,...
steps.instruction: Take 1 µL - 2 µL, combined eluate , vacuum dry and inject on MS to verify the digestion efficiency. Note: Analyse data with a 01:10:00 gradient run on QE HF-X or Orbitrap Lumos mass spectrometer in a FT-FT-HCD mode. Search data with Proteome Discoverer 2.1 or 2.4 version. Determine the digestion efficiency by plotting number of missed cleavages. Zero missed cleavages should be >75% and single missed cleavages should be between 20-23%.

steps.title: Vacuum dry the remaining peptide amount...
steps.instruction: Vacuum dry the remaining peptide amount and store in -80 °C deep freezer until ready to undertake TMT labelling.

steps.title: Dissolve 800 µg, of each of...
steps.instruction: Dissolve 800 µg, of each of the TMT mass tag reagents within the 11 or 16-plex TMT reagent kit with 41 µL, 100% by vol anhydrous acetonitrile to obtain a 20 µg/µL concentration for each TMT reporter tag.

steps.title: Leave them at Room temperature for 00:10:00
steps.instruction: Leave them at Room temperature for 00:10:00 . Following, vortex and briefly spin 2000 x g, °C, 2 minutes . Note: Dissolved TMT reagents are prone to hydrolysis so immediately after aliquoting store remainder reagent in -80 °C deep freezer for long-term storage up to six months and try to avoid multiple freeze thaw cycles.

steps.title: Dissolve lyophilized peptides in 50 µL...
steps.instruction: Dissolve lyophilized peptides in 50 µL of a mixture containing 38 µL, 50 mM TEAB buffer + 8 µL, 100% (by vol) anhydrous acetonitrile . Note: It is important to maintain a final 30 %, (by vol) of anhydrous Acetonitrile for an effective TMT reaction.

steps.title: Keep samples on a floater and...
steps.instruction: Keep samples on a floater and place it on a water bath sonicator for 00:10:00 .

steps.title: Centrifuge samples at high speed 20800...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge samples at high speed 20800 x g, °C, 10 minutes, room temperature .

steps.title: Transfer dissolved peptides into a 1
steps.instruction: Transfer dissolved peptides into a 1.5 ml protein low-binding eppendorf tube.

steps.title: Add 10 µL, 20 µg/µl TMT reagent i
steps.instruction: Add 10 µL, 20 µg/µl TMT reagent i.e. 200 µg aiming for a 1:1 mass ratio of peptide:TMT reagent.

steps.title: Give a gentle vortex and brief...
steps.instruction: Give a gentle vortex and brief spin 2000 x g, °C, 1 minute .

steps.title: Place samples on a Thermomixer and...
steps.instruction: Place samples on a Thermomixer and incubate with a gentle agitation at 800 rpm, °C, 2 hours, room temperature .

steps.title: Add another 50 µL, 50 mM...
steps.instruction: Add another 50 µL, 50 mM TEAB buffer to make a final 100 µL reaction. Vortex, brief spin at 2000 x g, °C, 1 minute and incubate on a Thermomixer for 00:10:00 . Note: It is a good practice to maintain the total volume to 100 µL final reaction as it helps in reducing pipetting error when aliquoting 5 µL of sample for label check efficiency).

steps.title: In order to verify the TMT...
steps.instruction: In order to verify the TMT labelling efficiency of each TMT mass tag, take a 5 µL, aliquot from each of the TMT samples and pool this in a single tube and vacuum dry immediately using a SpeedVac. Note: It is important to verify the labelling efficiency of each TMT mass tag and it should label > 98%, by analysing on Mass spec. We recommend doing this employing a 02:25:00 FT-FT-MS2 study. This will establish that each reporter tag is efficiently labelled and ensure that an equal level of each peptide is labelled with each of the TMT tags. Search MS raw data with Proteome Discoverer 2.2 or 2.4 by enabling TMTreporter tag mass (+229.163 Da) on Lysine residue and Peptide N-terminus as dynamic modifications. Filter TMT labelled Peptide spectral matches (PSMs) in the modification tab to calculate the number of labelled and unlabelled PSMs to determine the labelling efficiency. Also, export PSM abundance in txt.file, to plot a Boxplot using R-software to determine the ~1:1 abundance within and between replicates.

steps.title: Place remaining 95 µL of the...
steps.instruction: Place remaining 95 µL of the reaction in -80 °C freezer. If the labelling efficiency is >98% and levels of each labelled peptide appear to be close to 1:1 then proceed with the below steps.

steps.title: Thaw stored TMT labelled samples from...
steps.instruction: Thaw stored TMT labelled samples from step 46 to Room temperature .

steps.title: Prepare 5 %, (by vol) final...
steps.instruction: Prepare 5 %, (by vol) final Hydroxyl amine solution by dissolving in water from a 50 %, (by vol) stock solution .

steps.title: Add 5 µL, 5% (by vol)...
steps.instruction: Add 5 µL, 5% (by vol) Hydroxylamine to each sample to quench TMT reaction by incubating the reaction at Room temperature on a Thermomixer for 00:20:00 .

steps.title: Take 20% of the reaction i
steps.instruction: Take 20% of the reaction i.e. 220 µL to as a backup, snap freeze on dry ice and vacuum dry. Note: This is important because if there is a sample loss during the downstream analysis or to further validate the findings.

steps.title: Snap freeze the remaining 880 µL,...
steps.instruction: Snap freeze the remaining 880 µL, reaction and vacuum dry using Speed vac, for high pH fractionation.

steps.title: Desalt sample using tC18 50 mg...
steps.instruction: Desalt sample using tC18 50 mg cartridge by following the protocol described go to step # .

steps.title: After following C18 clean-up protocol, submit...
steps.instruction: After following C18 clean-up protocol, submit desalted sample to MS facility for high pH fractionation and request to fractionate to 96 fractions and concatenate by pooling distant fractions e.g A1+D1, A2+D2.. B1+E1, B2+E2 and so on to a total of 48 fractions for LC-MS/MS analysis. Note: Deep fractionation significantly reduces ion interference and ratio distortion caused by co-elution of peptides in turn and improves TMT-reporter ion quantification thus increasing the reliability of peptide and protein quantifications. Also, deep fractionation greatly improves the dynamic range of peptides/proteins and increases the coverage. We generally identify between 10,000 to 12,000 quantified protein groups.

steps.title: For each of the 48 fractions...
steps.instruction: For each of the 48 fractions measure peptide concentrations using a Nanodrop 1000 spectrophotometer.

steps.title: Prepare 2 µg, of each fraction...
steps.instruction: Prepare 2 µg, of each fraction in 15 µL, LC buffer (0.1% (by vol) formic acid in 3% (by vol) Acetonitrile) and submit each fraction to the mass spectrometry facility.

steps.title: Analyse each fraction by acquiring data...
steps.instruction: Analyse each fraction by acquiring data in FT-FT-FT (MS3) HCD mode on a Orbitrap Fusion Lumos Mass spectrometer for 01:25:00 run for each fraction.

steps.title: Prepare single layer of C18 stage-tip using 16-gauge syringe needle
steps.instruction: Prepare single layer of C18 stage-tip using 16-gauge syringe needle.

steps.title: Dissolve the vacuum dried peptides sample...
steps.instruction: Dissolve the vacuum dried peptides sample derived from go to step #45 in 80 µL, solvent-A1 (0.1% (by vol) TFA) .

steps.title: Add 80 µL, 100% (by vol)...
steps.instruction: Add 80 µL, 100% (by vol) Acetonitrile to the C18 stage-tip. Note: This is required to activate the C18 resin.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 2000 x g, °C, 2 minutes, room temperature
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 2000 x g, °C, 2 minutes, room temperature . Discard flow through.

steps.title: Add 80 µL, Solvent-A1 (0
steps.instruction: Add 80 µL, Solvent-A1 (0.1% (by vol) TFA) . This is required to equilibrate the C18 resin.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 2000 x g, °C, 2 minutes, room temperature
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 2000 x g, °C, 2 minutes, room temperature . Discard flow-through and repeat this step.

steps.title: Load the acidified peptide digest to Stage-tip
steps.instruction: Load the acidified peptide digest to Stage-tip.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 1500 x g, °C, 5 minutes, room temperature
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 1500 x g, °C, 5 minutes, room temperature .

steps.title: Reapply the flow through to the...
steps.instruction: Reapply the flow through to the C18 stage-tip column and repeat this step.

steps.title: Add 80 µL, solvent-A1 (0
steps.instruction: Add 80 µL, solvent-A1 (0.1% (by vol) TFA v/v) , and centrifuge at 2000 x g, °C, 2 minutes, room temperature . Discard flow through. Repeat again.

steps.title: Elute peptides absorbed to C18 column...
steps.instruction: Elute peptides absorbed to C18 column by placing the stage-tips into new 1.5 ml low binding tubes. Note: Using new tubes is important to avoid contamination.

steps.title: Add 30 µL, Elution buffer (Solvent-B1...
steps.instruction: Add 30 µL, Elution buffer (Solvent-B1 50% (by vol) acetonitrile in 0.1% (by vol) TFA) .

steps.title: Centrifuge at 1500 rpm, °C, 2 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 1500 rpm, °C, 2 minutes . Add another 30 µL, Elution buffer (Solvent-B1 50% (by vol) acetonitrile in 0.1% (by vol) TFA) and repeat the centrifugation.

steps.title: Immediately snap freeze on dry ice...
steps.instruction: Immediately snap freeze on dry ice the eluates and vacuum dry the samples using a SpeedVac.

steps.title: Submit to Mass spectrometry analysis as...
steps.instruction: Submit to Mass spectrometry analysis as described in go to step #45 .

steps.title: Dissolve vacuum dried pooled TMT sample...
steps.instruction: Dissolve vacuum dried pooled TMT sample from go to step #52 in 500 µL, solvent-A1 (0.1% (by vol) TFA) .

steps.title: Place sample on a floater and...
steps.instruction: Place sample on a floater and place it in on a water bath sonicator and sonicate it for 00:10:00 .

steps.title: Centrifuge sample at 20800 x g,...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge sample at 20800 x g, °C, 10 minutes at room temperature and transfer sample into a new 1.5 ml Eppendorf tube.

steps.title: Check that the pH of the...
steps.instruction: Check that the pH of the sample is acid >2, by pipetting 1 µL, sample onto a pH strip. Note: This is important because Stage-tips are equilibrated to acidic pH and this aid in proper binding of acidified peptides to C18 resin, if sample appears to be not acidic increase TFA concentration to 0.15 %, (by vol) and recheck pH.

steps.title: Prepare single tC18 50 mg Sep-Pak...
steps.instruction: Prepare single tC18 50 mg Sep-Pak cartridge and place this column in 15 ml falcon tube.

steps.title: Add 1 mL, 100% (by vol) Acetonitrile
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL, 100% (by vol) Acetonitrile . Centrifuge on an Eppendorf centrifuge at 60 x g, °C, 1 minute and discard flowthrough. Repeat this step again. Note: This step is required to activate the C18 resin.

steps.title: Equilibrate the tC18 50 mg Sep-Pak...
steps.instruction: Equilibrate the tC18 50 mg Sep-Pak cartridge, by adding 1 mL, solvent-A1 (0.1% (by vol) TFA v/v) . Centrifuge on an Eppendorf centrifuge at 60 x g, °C, 1 minute and discard flowthrough. Repeat this step two more times.

steps.title: Load acidified sample onto the tC18 50 mg Sep-Pak cartridge
steps.instruction: Load acidified sample onto the tC18 50 mg Sep-Pak cartridge. Note: Do not centrifuge and let the sample pass through by gravity or if needed push sample using rubber bulb (Fisher brand™ Rubber Pipette Bulb #12446180).

steps.title: Collect the flow through and reapply...
steps.instruction: Collect the flow through and reapply to the column and save the flow through. Note: If you don’t detect any peptides in MS, the flow through should have the peptides and this has been caused due to poor equilibration of the column.

steps.title: Wash the tC18 50 mg Sep-Pak...
steps.instruction: Wash the tC18 50 mg Sep-Pak cartridge by addition of 1 mL, solvent-A2 (0.1% (by vol) Formic acid) . Centrifuge on an Eppendorf centrifuge at 60 x g, °C, 1 minute and discard flowthrough. Repeat this step two more times.

steps.title: Place column on a new 1.5 ml Eppendorf tube. Note
steps.instruction: Place column on a new 1.5 ml Eppendorf tube. Note: Using new tubes is important to avoid contamination.

steps.title: Elute the peptides from the tC18...
steps.instruction: Elute the peptides from the tC18 50 mg Sep-Pak cartridge by addition of 300 µL, solventB2 (60% (by vol) Acetonitrile in 0.1% (by vol) Formic acid) . Note: Do not centrifuge and let the sample pass through by gravity or if needed push sample using rubber bulb (Fisher brand™ Rubber Pipette Bulb #12446180).

steps.title: Repeat elution step (step 84)
steps.instruction: Repeat elution step (step 84): Elute the peptides from the tC18 50 mg Sep-Pak cartridge by addition of 300 µL, solventB2 (60% (by vol) Acetonitrile in 0.1% (by vol) Formic acid) . Note: Do not centrifuge and let the sample pass through by gravity or if needed push sample using rubber bulb (Fisher brand™ Rubber Pipette Bulb #12446180).

steps.title: Repeat elution step (step 84)
steps.instruction: Repeat elution step (step 84): Elute the peptides from the tC18 50 mg Sep-Pak cartridge by addition of 300 µL, solventB2 (60% (by vol) Acetonitrile in 0.1% (by vol) Formic acid) . Note: Do not centrifuge and let the sample pass through by gravity or if needed push sample using rubber bulb (Fisher brand™ Rubber Pipette Bulb #12446180).

steps.title: Combine the 900 µL, eluate and...
steps.instruction: Combine the 900 µL, eluate and snap freeze and vacuum dry using SpeedVac.

steps.title: Store sample in -80 °C freezer...
steps.instruction: Store sample in -80 °C freezer or submit to mass spectrometry facility for high pH fractionation as described in go to step #54 .
